name: Marriage Certificate
description: An official declaration that two people are legally married, required for joint loans, passports, and name changes.
estimated_processing_time: Hindu Marriage Act: 15-30 days, Special Marriage Act: 60 days
eligibility: Groom must be 21+ and Bride must be 18+.
application_steps: Visit the State's e-District portal or the local Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) office.
Fill the application form, signed by both husband and wife.
Submit proofs of age, residence, and marriage (photos, invitation card).
Submit joint affidavits declaring marital status and nationality.
Both parties and witnesses must be present before the Registrar on the appointment day.
Certificate is issued after successful verification.

required_documents.name: Proof of Age (Both)
required_documents.where_to_get: Class 10th Certificate, Passport, or Birth Certificate

required_documents.name: Marriage Proofs
required_documents.purpose: Evidence that the marriage ceremony took place.
required_documents.where_to_get: Wedding Photos, Original Invitation Card, Priest's Certificate

required_documents.name: Witness ID Proofs
required_documents.purpose: To verify the identity of the witnesses.
required_documents.where_to_get: Aadhaar, PAN, Voter ID of witnesses

common_mistakes: Witnesses failing to appear on the appointment day.
Submitting a digital invitation card instead of a printed original.
Name mismatch in pre-marriage identity documents.
